data-cleanup-task.label: Data Cleanup Task
data-cleanup-task.summary: Use string functions and filtering to clean up data.

sketch2.title: Filtering Out Test and Draft Items

quiz-code-input-remove-test-items.title: SQL: Select Only Real, Active Items

sketch-intro-cleanup.title: Why Data Cleanup Matters
sketch-intro-cleanup.bodyMarkdown: Real-world data often contains inconsistencies, test entries, or outdated information. Cleaning up your data using SQL ensures that your analyses and reports are accurate. In this topic, you'll use the `inventory_items` table from the `inventory_ops` dataset to practice filtering out test items, standardizing text, and removing unnecessary records.

sketch-string-functions.title: Useful SQL String Functions
sketch-string-functions.bodyMarkdown:
SQL provides several string functions to help clean and standardize data:

- `UPPER(text)`: Converts text to uppercase.
- `LOWER(text)`: Converts text to lowercase.
- `TRIM(text)`: Removes leading and trailing spaces.
- `REPLACE(text, old, new)`: Replaces occurrences of a substring.

For example, to make all product names uppercase:

```sql
SELECT UPPER(name) FROM inventory_items;
```

sketch-filtering-rows.title: Filtering Out Test and Draft Items
sketch-filtering-rows.bodyMarkdown:
The `inventory_items` table includes a column `is_test` (1 for test items, 0 for real items) and a `status` column (like 'active', 'draft', or 'inactive'). To focus on real, active inventory, filter out rows where `is_test = 1` or `status` is not 'active'.

Example:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 0 AND status = 'active';
```

quiz-single-choice-cleanup-purpose.title: Purpose of Data Cleanup
quiz-single-choice-cleanup-purpose.prompt: Why is it important to clean up data in a database before running reports?
quiz-single-choice-cleanup-purpose.hint: Think about how test or draft data could affect your results.

help.concept: Data cleanup removes irrelevant or incorrect records, ensuring analyses reflect real business data.
help.hint_1: Test or draft entries can distort your reporting results.
help.hint_2: Accurate reports depend on using only real, up-to-date data.

options.a: To make the data look more colorful
options.b: To ensure reports are accurate and not affected by test or draft entries
options.c: To increase the number of rows in the table
options.d: To make SQL queries run slower

quiz-multi-choice-string-functions.title: String Functions for Cleanup
quiz-multi-choice-string-functions.prompt: Which of the following SQL functions can help standardize or clean up text data in a table?
quiz-multi-choice-string-functions.hint: Focus on functions that work with text, not numbers.

help.concept: String functions like UPPER, TRIM, and REPLACE help manipulate and standardize text data.
help.hint_1: SUM() is for numbers, not text.
help.hint_2: UPPER, TRIM, and REPLACE all work with text values.

quiz-drag-reorder-cleanup-steps.title: Order of Data Cleanup Steps
quiz-drag-reorder-cleanup-steps.prompt: Arrange these steps in the correct order for cleaning up inventory data.
quiz-drag-reorder-cleanup-steps.hint: Think about removing irrelevant data before formatting.

help.concept: Effective cleanup first removes unwanted rows, then standardizes the remaining data before reporting.
help.hint_2: You should only run the report after all cleanup steps are done.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-status-filter.title: Filtering by Status
quiz-fill-blank-choice-status-filter.prompt: To select only active inventory items, which value should you use in the WHERE clause for the status column?
quiz-fill-blank-choice-status-filter.hint: Look for the value that represents items currently available.

help.concept: The 'status' column indicates the current state of an item; 'active' means it's available.
help.hint_1: Only items with status 'active' are considered available.
help.hint_2: Use the value that matches available inventory.

quiz-code-input-remove-test-items.title: SQL: Select Only Real, Active Items
quiz-code-input-remove-test-items.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from `inventory_items` where the item is NOT a test item and has status 'active'.
quiz-code-input-remove-test-items.hint: Use WHERE to filter by is_test and status.

help.concept: Filtering rows with WHERE lets you exclude test items and focus on active inventory.
help.hint_1: Check that is_test is 0 and status is 'active'.
help.hint_2: Combine both conditions using AND in your WHERE clause.
